text: The surgeon begins dissection at the DJ flexure, proceeding deep to the inferior mesenteric vein. The dissection extends laterally towards the splenic flexure, exposing the left ureter in the process.
The surgeon exposes and skeletonizes the inferior mesenteric artery at the sacral promontory, entering the pre-sacral space at this level.

The surgeon completes the medial to lateral dissection by joining the dissections below and above the inferior mesenteric artery. The inferior mesenteric artery is fully skeletonized at its origin from the abdominal aorta.
The surgeon applies double clips to the inferior mesenteric artery and vein, then divides both vessels, completing their separation from surrounding tissues.

The surgeon continues medial to lateral dissection, exposing the left ureter. Concurrently, lateral to medial dissection begins at the pelvic brim and extends towards the splenic flexure, with the dissection spaces visibly joining.

The surgeon continues dissection on both the left and right sides of the rectum towards the pelvis. Posterior and pre-sacral dissection progresses in the total mesorectal excision (TME) plane. Anterior dissection involves dividing the peritoneal reflection in the Douglas pouch, advancing towards the pelvic floor.

The surgeon continues posterior dissection of the rectum towards the pelvic floor. The rectum is stapled and divided below the tumor using an endo-GIA tri-stapler, with multiple firings potentially required for complete division. The mesentery of the descending colon is divided at the level of the proximal resection.
The surgeon externalizes the resected specimen, completing the resection. A purse-string suture is applied, and an EEA circular stapler is introduced and opened for anastomosis.

The surgeon securely clicks the anvil into the circular stapler and closes the device. The coolant is checked for proper tension and rotation before firing the stapler. After firing, the stapler is extracted, and the anastomosis is tested underwater for leaks.
The surgeon inserts a tube drain and selects a loop of terminal ileum for the ileostomy. A washout of the surgical area is performed.
